project.title: Epitope and paratope mapping of a SUMO-remnant antibody using cross-linking mass spectrometry and molecular docking
project.projectDescription: The small ubiquitin-like modifier (SUMO) is an important post-translational modification that regulates the function of various proteins essential for DNA damage repair, genome integrity and cell homeostasis. To identify protein SUMOylation effectively, an enrichment step is necessary, often requires exogenous gene expression in cells and immunoaffinity purification of SUMO-remnant peptides following tryptic digestion. Previously, an antibody was developed to enrich tryptic peptides containing the remnant NQTGG on the receptor lysine, though the specifics of the structural interaction motif remained unclear. Here, we conducted de novo sequencing by mass spectrometry to analyze the SUMO-remnant antibody and performed paratope mapping using cross-linking experiments to identify key interacting residues within the complementarity-determining regions (CDRs). Additional cross-linking experiments were performed using SUMOylated peptides and high-field asymmetric waveform ion mobility spectrometry (FAIMS) to enhance sensitivity and confirm interactions at the paratope interface. Our comprehensive analyses have provided a detailed understanding of the structural interaction motifs of the SUMO-remnant antibody, offering valuable insights into the antibody’s specificity and binding mechanisms. This enhanced understanding paves the way for improved methodologies in studying protein SUMOylation and its regulatory roles in cellular processes.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Sample preparation protocol was an adaptation of Peng and al with a few modifications. Briefly, the SUMO-remnant antibody (Abcam, UMO-1-7-7) was denatured in 2% sodium deoxycholate (SDC), 200 mM Tris-HCl, and 10 mM Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP, Thermo Fisher Scientific), pH 8.0 at 95 °C for 10 min, followed by 30 min incubation at 37 °C. The sample was then alkylated with 20 mM 2-chloroacetamide (Aldrich). For selected proteolytic digestion, 3 µg of antibody was digested by one of the following proteases: trypsin (Promega), chymotrypsin (Promega), lysC (Wako), gluC (Promega), aspN (Promega), thermolysin (Promega), elastase (Promega) in a 1:50 ratio (w/w) in a total volume of 100 μL of 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate at 37 °C for 16 h. For sequential digestion, the antibody was first digested with trypsin for 16h, followed by the addition of chymotrypsin and incubation for 4h at 37 °C. For digestion with pepsin (Promega), 3 µg of antibody was digested at 37 °C, pH: 2.0 for 16 h. Vaccum dried peptides were resuspended in 4% FA and analysed by LC-MS/MS using an Easy nLC1000 coupled to an Orbitrap Fusion Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Desalted peptides or synthetic peptides containing NQTGG side chain were incubated overnight at 4°C with the SUMO-remnant antibody in PBS or immobilized on MagReSyn Protein A magnetic beads. The peptides/antibodies were then cross-linked with either, DSS, DSSO or TBDSPP (Thermo Fisher Scientific) at a final concentration of 2 mM.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: For de novo sequencing raw files were searched with PEAKS AB engine (Bioinformatics Solutions, Inc., Version 2.0). Parent and fragment mass tolerance were 12 ppm and 0.02 Da, respectively. A maximum of 8 variables modifications was allowed per peptide, and including oxidation (MHW), deamidation (NQ), carbamidomethylation (C) and Pyro-glu from Q. For intact mass analysis deconvolution was conducted using Thermo Biopharma Finder software 5.1 with the Xtract algorithm. For samples cross-linked with DSSO, raw files were searched with MaxLynx integrated into the MaxQuant environment. For sample cross-linked with TBDSPP data were analyzed using the XlinkX node of Proteome Discoverer 2.3. Finally, cross-linked samples with DSS were searched with pLink 2.

references.referenceLine: Comtois-Marotte S, Bonneil É, Li C, Smith MJ, Thibault P. Epitope and Paratope Mapping of a SUMO-Remnant Antibody Using Cross-Linking Mass Spectrometry and Molecular Docking.  J Proteome Res. 2025

Source Name: the SUMO-remnant antibody (UMO-1–7–7, available upon request to Abcam)
Characteristics[MaterialType]: the SUMO-remnant antibody (UMO-1–7–7, available upon request to Abcam)
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: Digestion was performed overnight with chymotrypsin at a 1:20 enzyme-to-peptide ratio.
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: TBDSPP-cross-linked peptides were enriched with a Titansphere Phos-TiO kit (GL Science, Tokyo, Japan).
Comment[Instrument]: LC-MS/MS analyses of cross-linked peptides were performed on an Orbitrap Exploris 480.
using a FAIMS Pro device (Thermo Fisher Scientific) operating at standard resolution mode (inner and outer electrodes maintained at 100 °C) with nitrogen as a carrier gas flowing at 4 L/min.
Comment[SpecilizedMS]: using a FAIMS Pro device (Thermo Fisher Scientific) operating at standard resolution mode (inner and outer electrodes maintained at 100 °C) with nitrogen as a carrier gas flowing at 4 L/min.
Comment[Separation]: gas-phase separation using high-field asymmetric waveform ion mobility spectrometry (FAIMS).
Comment[GradientTime]: a linear gradient of 10–100% solvent B (80% ACN, 0.2% FA) in 100 min
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: The isolation window was set to 1.2 Th with a 34% HCD normalized collision energy (NCE).
